name: Save applications with Google Drive and Microsoft Teams 24/7

parameters.formTitle: Save applications with Google Drive and Microsoft Teams 24/7

parameters.message: =✅ Save applications with Google Drive and Microsoft Teams 24/7 — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### New Lead Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Company, Score). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Download from Google Drive

### Download from Google Drive

Downloads the file's binary content from Google Drive so the next node can parse it.

Ports: ①→ Parse CSV Contents · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Parse CSV Contents

Parses the incoming CSV binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming lead records to a stable schema

Next: Rows Pass Validation?

### Rows Pass Validation?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.leadId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Load Rows into Google Sheets · ②→ Quarantine Bad Rows

### Quarantine Bad Rows

Appends a new row to 'Lead Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Load Rows into Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Lead Tracker', matched on `leadId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Repack as XLSX · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Load Rows into Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Repack as XLSX

Serializes items to a XLSX binary file.

Next: Archive Processed File (Google Drive)

### Archive Processed File (Google Drive)

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Fingerprint for Idempotency · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per lead (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Copy to Google Drive

### Archive Copy to Google Drive

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Archive Copy to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Lead Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Microsoft Teams · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#sales`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Lead Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Save applications with Google Drive and Microsoft Teams 24/7

**Pattern:** Form Intake · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Save applications with Google Drive and Microsoft Teams 24/7. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual outreach is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Lead Form Submission → Download from Google Drive → Parse CSV Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Rows Pass Validation? → Load Rows into Google Sheets → Repack as XLSX → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to form intake without manual effort
- Anyone automating outreach in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Bad rows are quarantined into their own store, not dropped silently and not allowed to break the whole batch — one malformed row in a 10,000-row file shouldn't stop the other 9,999 from loading, but it also shouldn't vanish without a trace.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Feed it a file with a few deliberately malformed rows and confirm they land in quarantine, not the main store.
2. Switch the source format (e.g. csv to xlsx) and see how little actually has to change.
3. Turn on archive mode and confirm the processed file gets repacked and stored.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your outreach process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
